description_long: This is an irregularly shaped, medium-sized stone with a rough, pitted surface and a predominantly gray coloration. The stone features a conical form with one end tapering to a blunt point and the other end broader and more angular, suggesting it may have fractured naturally. Its surface is marked by numerous small holes and textural variations, giving it a porous, volcanic appearance. One side displays a faint white marking or number, possibly for identification or cataloging. The stone's coloration ranges from light to dark gray, with subtle hints of brownish-red on the broader end, indicating some mineral variation or weathering. Overall, the stone appears dense and solid, suitable for use as an obstacle or barrier in an urban environment.
description: A rough, gray, conical stone with a pitted, porous surface and subtle brownish-red patches. It has an irregular shape and a dense, solid appearance.
description_view_0: This view highlights the stone's elongated, conical shape and the rough, pitted texture on its upper surface.
description_view_1: This side shows the stone's tapering end and a visible white marking or number on the surface.
description_view_2: The broader, more angular end of the stone is visible here, with increased surface roughness and hints of brownish-red coloration.
description_view_3: This view focuses on the blunt, fractured end of the stone, revealing deeper pits and a darker, weathered appearance.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or obstacle on the ground
attribute_pattern: Irregular pitting and porous texture with scattered small holes; no regular or artificial patterns.
attribute_weathering: Rough and pitted with visible signs of natural weathering, including minor discoloration and surface erosion.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with smooth, man-made surfaces but may partially blend with other natural stones or debris in urban settings.
